title: empogona ruandensis
intro: Empogona ruandensis, also known as the African white soapbark or white soapbark, is a plant species belonging to the Rubiaceae family. It is a shrub or small tree native to the tropical forests of central Africa. The medicinal properties of Empogona ruandensis lie in its bark, which contains saponins that have been traditionally used to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as to reduce fever and inflammation. The health benefits of the plant include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be harnessed through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves to promote skin health and relief from various ailments.
